column6: PackBits RLE8 BODY-chunk decoder: the while(number_of_bytes_written < line) loop reads one byte per iteration into rle_count (return value unchecked). When rle_count==128 (the PackBits noop opcode), neither the <128 literal-copy branch nor the >128 run-length branch fires, so number_of_bytes_written is never incremented. A crafted or malformed IFF file that continuously emits 0x80 bytes causes the loop to consume file bytes without advancing its termination variable, resulting in an infinite loop.
column9: Good new bug! Program never finishes if the file keeps feeding it 0x80 bytes

column6: ILBM interlaced PackBits unpacker: inner for(unsigned x=0; x<src_size;) loop has no automatic increment; x is only advanced inside the t>=0 or t!=-128 branches. When t==-128 (the documented noop), neither branch executes and x remains unchanged. A crafted ILBM stream with comp==1 that continuously emits 0x80 bytes keeps x==0 while advancing the file position by one byte per iteration, producing an infinite loop that exits only when the I/O source is exhausted or the process is killed.

column6: while(1) loop retries open() on EINTR (line 731: if(errno==EINTR) continue). If the fd is below SQLITE_MINIMUM_FILE_DESCRIPTOR=3, it closes the low fd, opens /dev/null to advance the fd counter, and retries — leaking one fd per retry. The EINTR-only path has no retry bound; if the process is repeatedly interrupted by signals without limit, the loop has no bounded retry count.
